ulf_hook: The earth is carved. The water fills the trench. The wild swine bow to the grain. Life begins in the mud. The old stones of the hall are mended. The mountain calls. The silver waits in the cold.
grandpa_legend: We’ve been busy getting the new farm at West-Thwaite ready, even if it meant a bit of a hitch-in-my-gitty-up digging that moat down to sea level. I spent a good while troubleshooting the airflow on the new X-frame chimney, but it was worth it to see the cooking station finally running. It warmed my old soul to see those two boars finally tame, especially with three new piglets already running around the pen. We had to fix up some structural damage at Othala from a previous raid, but the hearth is safe once more. Now, we're looking toward the Shadow-Garth Forge to start smelting our Frost-Tears. It’s time we finally moved into the Silver Age.
conrad_chronicle: The salt air bit at my skin as I swung the pickaxe into the dirt of Westshore, carving a deep moat until the cold sea water rushed in to claim the trench. I felt the weight of the Iron-Bark as I felled the great oak, its wood dense and stubborn against my blade. Inside the new walls of West-Thwaite, the scent of roasting meat finally replaced the stinging smoke once the X-frame chimney began to breathe. The taming is complete; the low grunts of the two beasts and the high squeals of three new lives offer a strange comfort against the silence of the wilds. I returned to Othala to find the scars of the Norns' Trial still etched into the timber, forcing me to tear down my pride and rebuild the ancestral walls. My hands are calloused, but there is no rest for the exile. I can feel the pull of the Shadow-Garth Forge, where the screaming snow of the mountain will meet the heat of the coals. The Frost-Tears wait for the flame. I will forge a new fate in the silver light.
